contextual.title: Sonographic Assessment and Visualization of Ectopics in Emergency Medicine
contextual.text:
Summary: Observational study of the use of ultrasound by emergency physicians in the evaluation of patients at risk of ectopic pregnancy.
Inclusion criteria: inclusion criteria: 

 pregnant females in 1st trimester 

 present to emergency department 

 complaint of abdominal/pelvic pain or vaginal bleeding 

 
Exclusion criteria: : 

 previous ultrasound diagnostic of location of pregnancy

metadata.brief_summary: Observational study of the use of ultrasound by emergency physicians in the evaluation of patients at risk of ectopic pregnancy.

Ectopic Pregnancy.definition: A potentially life-threatening condition in which EMBRYO IMPLANTATION occurs outside the cavity of the UTERUS. Most ectopic pregnancies (>96%) occur in the FALLOPIAN TUBES, known as TUBAL PREGNANCY. They can be in other locations, such as UTERINE CERVIX; OVARY; and abdominal cavity (PREGNANCY, ABDOMINAL).

A potentially life-threatening condition in which EMBRYO IMPLANTATION occurs outside the cavity of the UTERUS. Most ectopic pregnancies (>96%) occur in the FALLOPIAN TUBES, known as TUBAL PREGNANCY. They can be in other locations, such as UTERINE CERVIX; OVARY; and abdominal cavity (PREGNANCY, ABDOMINAL).
